module.name: Martial Arts Training
module.description: A comprehensive training module covering various martial arts disciplines.

martial_arts_baguazhang.name: Baguazhang
martial_arts_baguazhang.description: A Chinese martial art focused on circular movement, agility, and energy redirection.

martial_arts_capoeira.name: Capoeira
martial_arts_capoeira.description: A Brazilian martial art that combines acrobatics, dance, and fluid combat.

martial_arts_hapkido.name: Hapkido
martial_arts_hapkido.description: A Korean martial art that emphasizes joint locks, throws, and circular motion.

martial_arts_jeet_kune_do.name: Jeet Kune Do
martial_arts_jeet_kune_do.description: Bruce Lee’s philosophy-based martial art focused on efficiency, directness, and adaptability.

martial_arts_muay_thai.name: Muay Thai
martial_arts_muay_thai.description: A striking martial art from Thailand emphasizing powerful knee and elbow strikes.

martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu.name: Shaolin Kung Fu
martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu.description: A traditional Chinese martial art combining agility, strength, and internal discipline.

martial_arts_silat.name: Silat
martial_arts_silat.description: An Indonesian martial art known for its fluid striking, low stances, and blade techniques.

martial_arts_taekwondo.name: Taekwondo
martial_arts_taekwondo.description: A Korean martial art known for its high-speed kicking techniques and agility.

martial_arts_tai_chi.name: Tai Chi
martial_arts_tai_chi.description: An internal Chinese martial art focused on slow movements, energy flow, and balance.

martial_arts_wing_chun.name: Wing Chun
martial_arts_wing_chun.description: A close-quarters Chinese martial art emphasizing centerline strikes and deflections.

martial_arts_wushu.name: Wushu
martial_arts_wushu.description: A Chinese martial art known for its acrobatics, dynamic forms, and powerful striking techniques.

martial_arts_xing_yi_quan.name: Xing Yi Quan
martial_arts_xing_yi_quan.description: An internal Chinese martial art focused on linear, explosive strikes and five elemental movements.

martial_arts_baguazhang_circle_walking.name: Circle Walking
martial_arts_baguazhang_circle_walking.description: Master the core footwork pattern of Baguazhang to improve agility, balance, and energy flow.

cards.title: Basic Circle Walk
cards.description: A foundational technique for moving fluidly around an opponent while maintaining a defensive posture.
bulletpoints: Keep your posture upright and relaxed
Step with the heel first and roll onto the toes
Maintain consistent spacing between steps
cards.summaryText: Glide around your opponent with relaxed posture and even heel-to-toe steps.

cards.title: Direction Change Drill
cards.description: Learn how to shift directions smoothly while maintaining balance and fluidity.
bulletpoints: Pivot on the ball of your foot to change direction
Keep upper body steady while transitioning
Use coordinated breathing to maintain stability
cards.summaryText: Pivot cleanly while keeping the torso quiet and breath-led to stay balanced.

martial_arts_baguazhang_evasive_movement.name: Evasive Movement
martial_arts_baguazhang_evasive_movement.description: Train in evasive maneuvers that allow you to avoid attacks while maintaining offensive positioning.

cards.title: Shoulder Roll Dodge
cards.description: A defensive maneuver that utilizes circular shoulder movement to evade strikes.
bulletpoints: Roll the shoulder back to deflect incoming force
Use the motion to transition into a counterattack
Keep the movement smooth and fluid
cards.summaryText: Roll the shoulder away to slip the strike and coil for an immediate counter.

cards.title: Side Step and Sway
cards.description: A lateral evasion technique that keeps you within range for a counterstrike.
bulletpoints: Step off the centerline while maintaining balance
Use body rotation to follow up with a strike
Stay light on the feet for quick re-engagement
cards.summaryText: Blend a lateral step with a torso sway to stay in range for rapid counters.

martial_arts_baguazhang_palm_strikes.name: Palm Strikes
martial_arts_baguazhang_palm_strikes.description: Develop powerful and precise palm strikes used in Baguazhang for energy redirection and impact.

cards.title: Piercing Palm Strike
cards.description: A straight palm strike that focuses energy into a single point for penetration.
bulletpoints: Align the wrist with the forearm for structural integrity
Strike with the base of the palm for maximum force
Use a snapping motion to increase speed and impact
cards.summaryText: Drive a straight, snapping palm with an aligned wrist to pierce the guard.

cards.title: Spiral Palm Strike
cards.description: A rotating palm strike designed to slip through an opponent's defenses.
bulletpoints: Rotate the wrist as you extend the strike
Maintain a relaxed yet firm hand position
Follow through with body movement for added power
cards.summaryText: Spiral the palm through openings, letting relaxed rotation and body follow-through add power.

martial_arts_capoeira_acrobatics_and_fli.name: Acrobatics and Flips
martial_arts_capoeira_acrobatics_and_fli.description: Train in dynamic aerial movements that enhance agility, creativity, and control.

cards.title: Au (Capoeira Cartwheel)
cards.description: A fluid cartwheel-like motion used for evasion and attack positioning.
bulletpoints: Keep arms extended for balance
Engage core muscles to maintain control
Land softly and prepare for follow-up moves
cards.summaryText: Flow through the au with long arms, tight core control, and soft landings to reset fast.

cards.title: Macaco (Back Handspring Variation)
cards.description: A backward acrobatic flip that develops explosive power and transitions.
bulletpoints: Squat low before launching backward
Push off with one arm for stability
Spot the ground for a controlled landing
cards.summaryText: Launch the macaco from a low squat, drive with one arm, and keep eyes spotting the ground for a smooth catch.

martial_arts_capoeira_ginga_and_fluidity.name: Ginga and Fluidity
martial_arts_capoeira_ginga_and_fluidity.description: Develop the foundational movement of Capoeira, the Ginga, which allows fluid transitions between attacks and defenses.

cards.title: Basic Ginga Movement
cards.description: A fundamental side-to-side rocking motion used to maintain fluidity and readiness.
bulletpoints: Keep knees slightly bent for mobility
Move arms naturally for balance
Maintain a constant rhythm to the music
cards.summaryText: Rock through the ginga with soft knees, swinging arms, and steady rhythm to stay ready.

cards.title: Advanced Ginga Variations
cards.description: Enhance the Ginga by integrating directional shifts and feints.
bulletpoints: Use feints to mislead your opponent
Keep your center of gravity low for better stability
Practice transitioning smoothly between movements
cards.summaryText: Layer feints and low center control into your ginga so every transition stays smooth and deceptive.

martial_arts_capoeira_kicks_and_sweeps.name: Kicks and Sweeps
martial_arts_capoeira_kicks_and_sweeps.description: Develop powerful and deceptive kicks combined with low sweeps to control combat engagements.

cards.title: Meia Lua de Frente
cards.description: A half-moon kick that arcs across the opponent's body.
bulletpoints: Pivot on the standing foot for better rotation
Extend the leg smoothly to maintain control
Use hips to generate power
cards.summaryText: Arc the meia lua with a hip-driven pivot and smooth leg extension to slice across the guard.

cards.title: Rasteira (Leg Sweep)
cards.description: A low sweeping motion aimed at knocking an opponent off balance.
bulletpoints: Maintain a low center of gravity
Use hips to drive power into the sweep
Follow through with a return to standing position
cards.summaryText: Stay low for the rasteira, drive the hips through the sweep, and coil back to standing control.

martial_arts_hapkido_joint_locks.name: Joint Locks
martial_arts_hapkido_joint_locks.description: Learn the fundamental joint locking techniques in Hapkido used for control and submission.

cards.title: Basic Wrist Lock
cards.description: A technique used to control an opponent by applying pressure on the wrist joint.
bulletpoints: Use circular motion to apply the lock
Maintain close contact to limit opponent's movement
Apply gradual pressure to avoid injury
cards.summaryText: Roll into the wrist with tight contact and steady pressure to seize control safely.

cards.title: Elbow Hyperextension Lock
cards.description: A defensive technique that hyperextends the opponent’s elbow joint.
bulletpoints: Step to the side while applying pressure
Control the opponent’s wrist to limit escape
Use leverage rather than brute force
cards.summaryText: Angle off, capture the wrist, and lever the elbow straight for a clean hyperextension.

martial_arts_hapkido_pressure_points.name: Pressure Points
martial_arts_hapkido_pressure_points.description: Learn how to use pressure points to manipulate an opponent’s movement and cause temporary pain compliance.

cards.title: Wrist Pressure Point Control
cards.description: Applying targeted pressure on the wrist to force compliance.
bulletpoints: Use thumb or knuckle to apply pressure
Target the nerve cluster near the wrist joint
Combine with joint locks for greater effect
cards.summaryText: Dig into the wrist nerve with thumb or knuckle to amplify any lock or escort.

cards.title: Neck Pressure Point Strike
cards.description: A precise strike to the neck that causes temporary discomfort and disorientation.
bulletpoints: Strike with controlled force to avoid injury
Use the fingertips or the edge of the hand
Target the carotid artery or nerve points
cards.summaryText: Snap a precise edge-hand or fingertip strike into the neck points for brief disruption.

martial_arts_hapkido_throws_and_takedown.name: Throws and Takedowns
martial_arts_hapkido_throws_and_takedown.description: Master techniques for off-balancing and taking down an opponent with controlled force.

cards.title: Basic Hip Throw
cards.description: A fundamental throwing technique used to lift and flip an opponent over the hip.
bulletpoints: Get close to the opponent for maximum leverage
Rotate the hips to generate momentum
Control the fall to prevent injury
cards.summaryText: Close the distance, whip the hips, and steer the landing for a clean hip throw.

cards.title: Leg Sweep Takedown
cards.description: A sweeping motion used to trip an opponent off balance.
bulletpoints: Use a low stance for stability
Sweep the leg in a controlled manner
Follow up with a pin or submission
cards.summaryText: Sink low, carve the leg out smoothly, and flow straight into control after the sweep.

martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_footwork_and_s.name: Footwork and Speed
martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_footwork_and_s.description: Enhance mobility and speed to control distance and positioning in combat.

cards.title: Pendulum Step
cards.description: A fast, bouncing movement used to advance or retreat while staying balanced.
bulletpoints: Keep weight on the balls of your feet for quick movement
Use small, rapid steps rather than large leaps
Maintain constant motion to avoid becoming a static target
cards.summaryText: Bounce through pendulum steps on the balls of your feet to dash in or out without stalling.

cards.title: Angle Step Evasion
cards.description: A sidestepping maneuver used to evade attacks and create new angles of attack.
bulletpoints: Shift laterally instead of retreating backward
Keep hands up while moving to maintain defense
Use foot pivots to quickly change direction
cards.summaryText: Cut angles with lateral steps, guarded hands, and sharp pivots to open counter lanes.

martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_intercepting_f.name: Intercepting Fist
martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_intercepting_f.description: Master Bruce Lee’s concept of attacking while the opponent initiates an attack, maximizing speed and efficiency.

cards.title: Straight Lead Punch
cards.description: A fast and direct punch designed to intercept an opponent's attack.
bulletpoints: Extend the arm directly forward with minimal movement
Strike from the centerline for maximum efficiency
Use explosive speed and minimal telegraphing
cards.summaryText: Snap the straight lead from centerline with zero telegraph to meet the attack mid-launch.

cards.title: Stop-Hit Counter
cards.description: A defensive counter that disrupts an incoming attack before it lands.
bulletpoints: Time the strike just as the opponent initiates movement
Use the least amount of force necessary for interruption
Follow up immediately with an offensive combination
cards.summaryText: Intercept with a stop-hit the instant they move, then flow into your finishing combo.

martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_trapping_hands.name: Trapping Hands
martial_arts_jeet_kune_do_trapping_hands.description: Develop hand control techniques that allow rapid offense and defense within close-range combat.

cards.title: Pak Sao (Slap Block)
cards.description: A redirection technique that deflects an opponent’s attack to create an opening.
bulletpoints: Use the palm to deflect an incoming strike sideways
Maintain relaxed shoulders for quicker follow-up
Transition into an immediate counterattack
cards.summaryText: Smack the attack off-line with a relaxed pak sao and fire your counter instantly.

cards.title: Lop Sao (Grab and Pull)
cards.description: A technique used to trap and pull an opponent’s arm, disrupting their attack.
bulletpoints: Grip the opponent’s wrist firmly while pulling
Keep elbows close to the body for better leverage
Follow up with a counterstrike or takedown
cards.summaryText: Clamp the wrist with lop sao, pull tight to trap, and finish with your chosen follow-up.

martial_arts_muay_thai_clinch_and_contro.name: Clinch and Control
martial_arts_muay_thai_clinch_and_contro.description: Develop close-range control techniques to dominate an opponent in the clinch.

cards.title: Double Collar Tie
cards.description: A dominant clinch position that allows control over the opponent’s head movement.
bulletpoints: Grip the back of the opponent’s head with both hands
Keep elbows close together to maintain control
Use head movement to prevent counterattacks
cards.summaryText: Lock the double collar tie tight with closing elbows and steer their head to smother counters.

cards.title: Inside Clinch Control
cards.description: A positioning technique that allows control over the opponent’s upper body while avoiding strikes.
bulletpoints: Position one arm under the opponent’s shoulder
Use hip movement to stay balanced
Look for opportunities to land knee strikes
cards.summaryText: Swim inside the clinch, ride the hips for balance, and punctuate control with sharp knees.

martial_arts_muay_thai_elbow_and_knee_st.name: Elbow and Knee Strikes
martial_arts_muay_thai_elbow_and_knee_st.description: Master powerful and devastating close-range strikes using elbows and knees.

cards.title: Horizontal Elbow Strike
cards.description: A fast and sharp elbow strike aimed at cutting through an opponent’s defense.
bulletpoints: Keep elbow level with the target for maximum impact
Pivot on the lead foot to generate power
Aim for the temple, jaw, or ribs
cards.summaryText: Slice the horizontal elbow with a pivoted hip to carve through head or body guards.

cards.title: Straight Knee Strike
cards.description: A powerful knee strike delivered to the opponent’s midsection.
bulletpoints: Thrust the hips forward to drive the knee
Keep the foot flexed to avoid injury
Use the clinch for better control and positioning
cards.summaryText: Drive a clinched straight knee by thrusting hips forward and keeping the foot tight.

martial_arts_muay_thai_power_kicks.name: Power Kicks
martial_arts_muay_thai_power_kicks.description: Develop explosive and powerful kicks that can deal significant damage in a fight.

cards.title: Roundhouse Kick
cards.description: A signature Muay Thai kick delivered with full-body rotation for maximum force.
bulletpoints: Pivot on the supporting foot for power
Strike with the shin rather than the foot
Follow through to maintain balance
cards.summaryText: Whip the roundhouse by pivoting hard, driving the shin, and letting momentum carry through.

cards.title: Low Leg Kick
cards.description: A powerful kick aimed at the opponent’s thigh to slow them down.
bulletpoints: Aim for the inner or outer thigh
Use the hips to generate force
Quickly reset stance to avoid counters
cards.summaryText: Chop the low kick into the thigh with hip drive, then snap back before they counter.

martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_animal_styl.name: Animal Styles
martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_animal_styl.description: Train in the legendary animal-inspired Shaolin techniques, each emphasizing a different combat strategy.

cards.title: Tiger Claw
cards.description: A powerful technique that mimics the crushing force of a tiger’s strike.
bulletpoints: Use an open-hand grip with fingers slightly curved
Strike with the palm while raking with fingers
Target pressure points like the face, throat, or ribs
cards.summaryText: Clamp a tiger claw with curved fingers to crush in and rake vital targets.

cards.title: Crane Beak Strike
cards.description: A precision-based attack targeting soft tissue areas.
bulletpoints: Form the fingers into a beak-like shape
Strike upward with the tips of the fingers
Use fluid movements to maintain agility
cards.summaryText: Shape the crane beak, spear upward into soft tissue, and keep your flow light.

martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_iron_body_t.name: Iron Body Training
martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_iron_body_t.description: Develop endurance and conditioning through traditional Shaolin iron body techniques.

cards.title: Iron Palm Conditioning
cards.description: A training method to toughen the hands for striking without injury.
bulletpoints: Practice slapping sandbags for increased palm strength
Use progressive conditioning to avoid long-term damage
Focus on proper breathing to channel energy
cards.summaryText: Harden the palms with gradual sandbag strikes while breathing to protect the joints.

cards.title: Qigong Breath Control
cards.description: A breathing exercise to enhance internal energy and resilience.
bulletpoints: Inhale deeply through the nose and expand the diaphragm
Exhale slowly while focusing on energy circulation
Maintain a relaxed but powerful posture
cards.summaryText: Cycle deep qigong breaths to circulate energy and reinforce whole-body resilience.

martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_staff_and_w.name: Staff and Weaponry
martial_arts_shaolin_kung_fu_staff_and_w.description: Master traditional Shaolin weapons, including the staff and swords.

cards.title: Basic Staff Spin
cards.description: A foundational movement for controlling the Shaolin staff with speed and accuracy.
bulletpoints: Keep a loose but firm grip for maximum rotation speed
Practice switching hand positions smoothly
Use wrist movement to generate momentum
cards.summaryText: Spin the staff with relaxed control, quick hand switches, and wrist-driven momentum.

cards.title: Broadsword Slash
cards.description: A powerful, sweeping attack using the Shaolin broadsword.
bulletpoints: Lead with the edge of the sword for cutting efficiency
Maintain a fluid transition between strikes
Use body rotation to generate additional power
cards.summaryText: Let the broadsword edge flow through sweeping cuts powered by whole-body rotation.

martial_arts_silat_bladed_weapon_techniq.name: Bladed Weapon Techniques
martial_arts_silat_bladed_weapon_techniq.description: Learn efficient and deadly techniques for using knives and short blades in combat.

cards.title: Reverse Grip Slash
cards.description: A cutting technique using a reverse grip to create close-quarters control.
bulletpoints: Hold the blade in a downward grip
Use quick, slicing motions to target limbs
Keep the blade close to the body for control
cards.summaryText: Ride the reverse grip tight to your frame and carve quick limb-level slashes.

cards.title: Deflect and Stab Counter
cards.description: A defensive maneuver that redirects an attack and follows up with a stab.
bulletpoints: Parry the incoming strike using a small circular motion
Step off the centerline to avoid counterattacks
Follow up with a precise thrust to a vital area
cards.summaryText: Slip the attack with a tight parry, sidestep, and drive a pinpoint follow-up stab.

martial_arts_silat_low_stance_combat.name: Low Stance Combat
martial_arts_silat_low_stance_combat.description: Master Silat’s unique low stances that improve balance, mobility, and evasiveness.

cards.title: Squat Evade and Counter
cards.description: A defensive movement that allows quick evasions followed by counterattacks.
bulletpoints: Drop into a deep squat to avoid high attacks
Keep weight evenly distributed for fast transitions
Use hand strikes while rising for added power
cards.summaryText: Sink into the squat to slip high shots, then spring up with strikes on the rebound.

cards.title: Low Leg Sweep
cards.description: A sweeping attack targeting an opponent’s base to take them off balance.
bulletpoints: Stay low and use a circular sweeping motion
Engage hips to generate more power
Follow up immediately to prevent recovery
cards.summaryText: Glide a low sweep with hip drive to rip their base and surge into the next attack.

martial_arts_silat_rapid_striking.name: Rapid Striking
martial_arts_silat_rapid_striking.description: Develop lightning-fast strikes that overwhelm opponents through speed and precision.

cards.title: Straight Punch Chain
cards.description: A rapid combination of straight punches used to break through an opponent’s guard.
bulletpoints: Keep punches compact for speed
Maintain a strong centerline position
Focus on precision rather than brute force
cards.summaryText: Drill tight straight punches down the centerline to overwhelm faster than they can reset.

cards.title: Palm Strike Blitz
cards.description: A rapid sequence of open-hand palm strikes aimed at soft targets.
bulletpoints: Use the base of the palm to strike
Target the chin, nose, or solar plexus
Chain multiple strikes together for continuous pressure
cards.summaryText: Blast rapid palm strikes into soft targets with the heel of the hand to keep pressure on.

martial_arts_taekwondo_breaking_techniqu.name: Breaking Techniques
martial_arts_taekwondo_breaking_techniqu.description: Learn how to generate force and precision for board and brick breaking in Taekwondo.

cards.title: Palm Strike Break
cards.description: A forceful palm strike used for breaking wooden boards.
bulletpoints: Strike with the base of the palm
Use a snapping motion for maximum power
Follow through the target rather than stopping at impact
cards.summaryText: Snap the palm through the board with the heel of the hand and a full follow-through.

cards.title: Axe Kick Board Break
cards.description: A downward striking kick designed for breaking hard objects.
bulletpoints: Raise the leg high before driving downward
Strike with the heel for maximum force
Engage the core to control balance and stability
cards.summaryText: Lift the axe kick tall, hammer the heel straight down, and brace with the core for the break.

martial_arts_taekwondo_explosive_kicking.name: Explosive Kicking
martial_arts_taekwondo_explosive_kicking.description: Train powerful and high-speed kicks that maximize impact and efficiency.

cards.title: Front Snap Kick
cards.description: A quick and powerful kick aimed at the opponent’s midsection or head.
bulletpoints: Chamber the knee before extending the leg
Snap the foot forward for a sharp impact
Retract quickly to maintain balance
cards.summaryText: Pop the front snap kick by chambering tight, snapping out, and recoiling instantly.

cards.title: Roundhouse Kick
cards.description: A powerful circular kick aimed at the ribs or head.
bulletpoints: Pivot on the standing foot to generate torque
Keep the leg extended throughout the motion
Follow through for maximum force
cards.summaryText: Whip the roundhouse with a hard pivot and full follow-through to blast the target.

martial_arts_taekwondo_spinning_and_aeri.name: Spinning and Aerial Kicks
martial_arts_taekwondo_spinning_and_aeri.description: Develop high-flying, spinning, and acrobatic kicks for advanced Taekwondo techniques.

cards.title: Tornado Kick
cards.description: A spinning kick that generates speed and power through rotation.
bulletpoints: Step forward and pivot for momentum
Use the rear leg to execute a full spin
Strike with the instep or shin for impact
cards.summaryText: Whip through the tornado kick with a driving step, full spin, and sharp instep impact.

cards.title: Jumping Back Kick
cards.description: A counterattack kick that delivers a powerful blow while retreating.
bulletpoints: Jump backward while rotating the hips
Kick straight back with a fully extended leg
Land smoothly to maintain control
cards.summaryText: Launch the jumping back kick by hopping back, firing the heel straight out, and sticking the landing.

martial_arts_tai_chi_internal_energy.name: Internal Energy
martial_arts_tai_chi_internal_energy.description: Training methods to develop and control the internal energy (Qi) within Tai Chi practice.

cards.title: Qigong Breathing
cards.description: A foundational breathing technique for cultivating internal energy.
bulletpoints: Inhale deeply through the nose, expanding the diaphragm
Exhale slowly and visualize energy flowing through the body
Maintain a calm and steady rhythm
cards.summaryText: Cycle deep qigong breaths with calm visualization to smooth qi through the body.

cards.title: Dan Tian Energy Focus
cards.description: A meditative practice for concentrating energy in the lower abdomen.
bulletpoints: Place hands over the lower abdomen and focus on warmth
Use circular hand motions to guide internal energy
Coordinate slow movements with deep breathing
cards.summaryText: Center awareness in the dan tian, circling the hands and breath to gather warmth.

martial_arts_tai_chi_push_hands.name: Push Hands
martial_arts_tai_chi_push_hands.description: A sensitivity training exercise to develop balance, coordination, and energy flow.

cards.title: Basic Push Hands
cards.description: A foundational Tai Chi drill that teaches sensitivity and redirection.
bulletpoints: Maintain a relaxed posture and stable stance
Use circular hand movements to redirect energy
Focus on feeling the opponent's force rather than resisting it
cards.summaryText: Stay relaxed in push hands, circling the arms to feel and divert incoming force.

cards.title: Single-Hand Push Hands Drill
cards.description: A variation that isolates one hand to refine coordination and control.
bulletpoints: Focus on using one hand to maintain contact
Practice maintaining balance while pivoting
Use breath control to enhance relaxation and fluidity
cards.summaryText: Work the single-hand drill by keeping light contact, pivoting smoothly, and breathing ease.

martial_arts_tai_chi_slow_form_training.name: Slow Form Training
martial_arts_tai_chi_slow_form_training.description: Step-by-step guidance on practicing slow, deliberate Tai Chi forms to improve flow and mindfulness.

cards.title: Ward Off Movement
cards.description: A fundamental movement that builds stability and control.
bulletpoints: Shift weight smoothly between both legs
Extend one arm while maintaining a rounded posture
Keep movements continuous and uninterrupted
cards.summaryText: Glide through ward off with fluid weight shifts, rounded arms, and unbroken flow.

cards.title: Grasp the Bird’s Tail
cards.description: A combination of Tai Chi movements incorporating balance and coordination.
bulletpoints: Move through each transition slowly and with intent
Maintain a relaxed yet controlled posture
Use breath to guide each movement
cards.summaryText: Ease through grasp the bird’s tail, letting breath pace each balanced transition.

martial_arts_wing_chun_centerline_striki.name: Centerline Striking
martial_arts_wing_chun_centerline_striki.description: Develop precise and rapid strikes along the centerline for maximum efficiency.

cards.title: Chain Punching
cards.description: A continuous series of rapid punches aimed at overwhelming the opponent.
bulletpoints: Keep punches compact and aligned along the centerline
Use wrist relaxation to increase speed
Maintain forward pressure without overextending
cards.summaryText: Fire chain punches straight down the line with relaxed wrists and relentless drive.

cards.title: Vertical Palm Strike
cards.description: A palm-heel strike aimed at delivering controlled force while protecting the hands.
bulletpoints: Keep the palm vertical and fingers pointed upward
Strike with the base of the palm for added stability
Engage the hips to generate extra power
cards.summaryText: Shoot the vertical palm with the heel of the hand while the hips add compact power.

martial_arts_wing_chun_chi_sao_sensitivi.name: Chi Sao Sensitivity
martial_arts_wing_chun_chi_sao_sensitivi.description: Develop tactile reflexes and sensitivity through Wing Chun’s signature Chi Sao (Sticky Hands) drills.

cards.title: Single-Hand Chi Sao
cards.description: A fundamental drill to build sensitivity and energy flow in close combat.
bulletpoints: Maintain relaxed yet responsive arm contact
Focus on feeling the opponent’s energy rather than brute force
Use circular hand movements to deflect incoming strikes
cards.summaryText: Roll a single arm to read pressure changes and redirect strikes without muscling through.

cards.title: Double-Hand Chi Sao
cards.description: An advanced variation that integrates both hands to develop reflexive counterattacks.
bulletpoints: Maintain centerline control while engaging both hands
Use light, controlled pressure to manipulate the opponent
Flow between offense and defense seamlessly
cards.summaryText: Let both arms cycle with soft pressure so counterattacks fire the moment a line opens.

martial_arts_wing_chun_wooden_dummy_dril.name: Wooden Dummy Drills
martial_arts_wing_chun_wooden_dummy_dril.description: Train precise strikes, deflections, and footwork using the Wing Chun wooden dummy.

cards.title: Basic Dan Gee Drill
cards.description: A fundamental drill for practicing proper hand positioning and deflection angles.
bulletpoints: Strike with controlled force, not brute power
Keep elbows down to maintain Wing Chun structure
Flow from one technique to the next smoothly
cards.summaryText: Thread the dummy arms with relaxed structure so each strike snaps back on line.

cards.title: Lop Sao & Gan Sao Sequence
cards.description: A sequence using pulling and deflecting techniques to control the opponent.
bulletpoints: Use the Lop Sao (grabbing hand) to redirect attacks
Follow up with Gan Sao (low sweeping deflection)
Maintain strong root and balance throughout
cards.summaryText: Catch with Lop Sao, sweep with Gan Sao, and stay rooted as the dummy arm cycles back.

martial_arts_wushu_aerial_movements.name: Aerial Movements
martial_arts_wushu_aerial_movements.description: Develop acrobatic jumping techniques to enhance agility and combat versatility.

cards.title: Butterfly Kick
cards.description: A signature Wushu move combining aerial grace with combat effectiveness.
bulletpoints: Lean forward and sweep the legs in a circular motion
Generate lift by pushing off with both feet
Use arm movement to stabilize in midair
cards.summaryText: Drive the butterfly kick by leaning through the sweep and floating level with steady arms.

cards.title: 540 Kick
cards.description: An advanced spinning kick requiring exceptional control and explosiveness.
bulletpoints: Initiate a strong jump with a full-body rotation
Focus on tucking the legs for additional spin
Land softly with proper weight distribution
cards.summaryText: Launch the 540 with a tight tuck, snap the strike midair, then melt into a quiet landing.

martial_arts_wushu_dynamic_forms.name: Dynamic Forms
martial_arts_wushu_dynamic_forms.description: Train in the fluid, high-speed movements of Wushu forms for agility and precision.

cards.title: Chain Punching Combination
cards.description: A rapid sequence of punches designed to overwhelm opponents with speed.
bulletpoints: Keep movements continuous and fluid
Ensure minimal telegraphing for faster execution
Focus on maintaining centerline control
cards.summaryText: Let the chain punches stream straight ahead so speed and alignment crowd the guard.

cards.title: Tornado Kick Combo
cards.description: A spinning kick sequence that blends power with acrobatics.
bulletpoints: Generate momentum through a full-body rotation
Keep the supporting foot grounded for balance
Follow through for maximum impact
cards.summaryText: Whip the tornado kick with a tall pivot, snap the strike mid-arc, and flow through the landing.

martial_arts_wushu_long_fist_and_souther.name: Long Fist and Southern Fist
martial_arts_wushu_long_fist_and_souther.description: Master both the extended-range strikes of Long Fist and the powerful stances of Southern Fist.

cards.title: Long Fist Combination
cards.description: A flowing series of extended strikes and evasive maneuvers.
bulletpoints: Keep strikes loose yet controlled for maximum reach
Incorporate low stances for balance and fluidity
Ensure quick recovery for the next movement
cards.summaryText: Whip the long fist combo from deep stances so each reach snaps back ready for the next line.

cards.title: Southern Fist Power Strike
cards.description: A rooted, explosive strike emphasizing strength and impact.
bulletpoints: Generate power from the hips and core
Keep a low, stable stance for balance
Focus on decisive execution with each movement
cards.summaryText: Sink the stance, drive from the hips, and drop the southern fist with decisive weight.

martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_animal_forms.name: Animal Forms
martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_animal_forms.description: Master Xing Yi’s animal-inspired techniques for agility and striking power.

cards.title: Tiger Form
cards.description: A ferocious forward-driving attack that embodies the strength of a tiger.
bulletpoints: Sink into a low stance for power
Use both hands in simultaneous claw-like strikes
Generate explosive force from the hips
cards.summaryText: Prowl low, rip forward with paired claws, and let the hips drive the tiger’s surge.

cards.title: Eagle Form
cards.description: A long-range technique that focuses on precision strikes and quick movement.
bulletpoints: Use sweeping arm movements to control space
Keep a light and agile stance
Strike with focused fingertips targeting pressure points
cards.summaryText: Glide with wide wings, stay springy, and spear pressure points with eagle talons.

martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_explosive_powe.name: Explosive Power
martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_explosive_powe.description: Train internal energy and whole-body coordination to generate maximum force.

cards.title: Fa Jin Training
cards.description: A practice for developing sudden explosive energy release.
bulletpoints: Relax the muscles before each strike
Generate power from the ground up
Snap movements quickly to create an explosive effect
cards.summaryText: Soften first, then drive fa jin from the floor and crack it out in a single beat.

cards.title: Coiling Force Application
cards.description: A technique that stores energy through coiling motions before release.
bulletpoints: Twist the torso to build up kinetic energy
Release power through a snapping motion
Maintain fluidity between transitions
cards.summaryText: Spiral the torso to store energy, then uncoil in one smooth snap without stiffness.

martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_five_element_s.name: Five Element Strikes
martial_arts_xing_yi_quan_five_element_s.description: Train the five core striking techniques based on the five elements in Xing Yi Quan.

cards.title: Pi Quan (Splitting Strike)
cards.description: A downward splitting strike representing the element of Metal.
bulletpoints: Drive the force downward in a cutting motion
Maintain a strong stance for stability
Focus on speed and fluidity between movements
cards.summaryText: Let Pi Quan fall like a blade, rooted stance steady while the cut stays fluid.

cards.title: Beng Quan (Crushing Strike)
cards.description: A forward-driving punch that embodies the element of Wood.
bulletpoints: Extend the punch in a straight line with force
Keep the shoulders relaxed for speed
Use the rear foot to generate forward momentum
cards.summaryText: Drive Beng Quan straight ahead with a relaxed frame and rear-foot surge.
